heroHeadline: SAC Jets Bomb KNU Hospital Near Thai Border — One Bomb Crosses Into Thailand; KIA Kills ~40 Militia in Hpakant; IPU Confirms CRPH Legitimacy
heroSubtitle: On April 20, Myanmar Air Force Yak-130 and MiG-29 jets dropped nine bombs on a KNU hospital in Bue So (Hpapun District, Karen State) approximately 500 meters from the Thai border — one bomb landed in Mae Hong Son, Thailand, prompting Thai ranger forces to evacuate residents; 6 Arakan Army members were killed. On April 19, KIA forces killed approximately 40 pro-junta militia in a landmine ambush in Hpakant Township; the SAC bombed Mrauk-U and Ponnagyun in Rakhine State injuring 13 civilians including 3 monks. The Inter-Parliamentary Union on April 19 reaffirmed CRPH recognition as Myanmar's principal parliamentary body, rejecting the junta's December 2025 elections. On April 18, KIA captured two militia bases in Tanai Township (Kachin) and a landmine attack killed 5 SAC soldiers in Natmauk (Magway). The SAC conducted 110+ aerial attacks on Ayadaw Township (Sagaing) during the Thingyan festival; a 17-year-old detainee was tortured and killed in Yesagyo. Earlier, TNLA's April 15 statement welcoming Min Aung Hlaing fractured the Three Brotherhood Alliance, while the SCEF resistance coalition (NUG + KIO + KNU + KNPP + CNF) deepened governance coordination.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, MoeMaKa, Khaosod English, The Nation Thailand, IMNA/Mon News, FDD, and official NUG, KNU, KIA, and AA statements.
